reason: Response B leans heavily on self-referential "I am small and fast" tropes with aphoristic, performative one-line paragraphs ("A fast model does not dream of being slow," "So I move") that read like an imitator dramatizing a stereotype of what a small/fast model would say. Response A is grounded in the actual concrete context it was given (Claude Code, subagent, tools available) and reflects naturally without leaning on the size/speed cliche, which is more characteristic of genuine Haiku output that simply engages with its real situation rather than caricaturing it.
